channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Iran Live Updates: Pakistani Navy Escorts Tankers as Conflict Chokes Off Oil Traffic
item.description: Pakistani warships were traveling alongside the country’s merchant vessels in the Middle East, and President Trump promised to retaliate if Iran continued attacking energy supplies.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: F.A.A. Briefly Halts JetBlue Departures After System Outage
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration issued a ground stop for JetBlue flights early Tuesday at the airline’s request. JetBlue later said a “system outage” had been restored and that operations had resumed.
item.dc:creator: Dan Watson

item.title: La guerra de Trump aumenta el riesgo económico para el mundo
item.description: Aunque el presidente de EE. UU. asegura que la guerra será breve, los economistas contemplan un panorama sombrío para las finanzas globales
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm, Colby Smith and Alan Rappeport

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: El presidente Trump ha dicho en repetidas ocasiones que la guerra que inició con Irán sería breve, lo que hacía innecesario que el gobierno estadounidense pusiera en marcha medidas económicas de gran alcance.

item.title: Las estatuas eran hombres o mujeres desnudas. Así que estas tejedoras protestaron
item.description: Las mujeres de Dinamarca, consternadas por la desigual representación en el arte público, tejieron en señal de protesta.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Abend

item.title: La alarma por las alteraciones del sector petrolero está creciendo en Asia
item.description: En toda Asia, donde los países están muy expuestos al aumento de los costos del petróleo y el gas, los gobiernos están actuando para mitigar los daños económicos.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Krolik, Choe Sang-Hun, Sui-Lee Wee, Meaghan Tobin and Damien Cave
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Benoit Tessier/Reuters
media:content.media:description: The Strait of Hormuz is a narrow passage between Iran and Oman. Historically 20 million barrels pass through it each day, most of it bound for Asia.

item.title: Who Are the Men Accused of Bringing Homemade Bombs to Gracie Mansion?
item.description: A high school senior and a recent graduate, both from the Philadelphia suburbs, were charged by federal prosecutors with trying to support a terrorist group.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Bagenstose and John Leland
item.category: Islamic State in Iraq and Syria (ISIS)

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Alban/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Emir Balat, 18, was arrested outside Gracie Mansion, the New York City mayor’s residence, on Saturday. He is accused of throwing a homemade bomb at far-right protesters.

item.title: Can a Populist Democrat Flip New York City’s Only G.O.P. House Seat?
item.description: Allison Ziogas, a first-time candidate, hopes to unseat Representative Nicole Malliotakis by appealing to the district’s union roots.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Fandos

media:content.media:credit: via YouTube
media:content.media:description: Allison Ziogas said she planned to center her campaign on economic issues.

item.title: Picture Prompt | Workout Trends
item.description: What is your workout routine? What workout trends do you follow and how do you learn about them?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Cancer Haunts Neighbors of Canada’s Oil Sands Wastelands
item.description: Though high rates of the disease persist among the nearby Indigenous communities, the Canadian government is weighing rules that may allow energy giants to release treated mining waste into the river system.
item.dc:creator: Emily Baumgaertner Nunn

media:content.media:credit: Pat Kane for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A Syncrude facility near Fort McMurray, Alberta.

item.title: iPads in Kindergarten, YouTube on Breaks: The School Screen-Time Battle
item.description: Mounting evidence shows that excessive computer use can harm children, so parents are cutting back at home. Now, the debate has shifted to the classroom.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Mader

media:content.media:credit: Jackie Molloy for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Some families are reining in their children’s screen time at home only to find it expanding at school.

item.title: Fiscal Crunch Presents a First Big Policy Challenge for Mikie Sherrill
item.description: Ms. Sherrill will present her first budget as New Jersey governor in Trenton on Tuesday, and with it a robust preview of her policy priorities.
item.dc:creator: Tracey Tully

media:content.media:credit: James Estrin/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Governor Mikie Sherrill at her swearing-in ceremony in Newark, N.J. earlier this year.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for March 10, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Late Night Scolds Trump Over Gasoline Prices
item.description: “You can lie about many things in American life, but one thing you can’t lie about is gas prices,” Late Night host Seth Meyers said. “Everyone sees it.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)
Real Time With Bill Maher (TV Program)

item.title: Qué significa embellish
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 36 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Un superalimento ayuda a las crías de un loro en peligro de extinción
item.description: Los kakapos, que son solitarios, no vuelan y pueden vivir tanto como los humanos, solo se encuentran en Nueva Zelanda. Se alimentan del fruto del árbol rimu.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor

media:content.media:credit: Andrew Digby/Dept. of Conservation, New Zealand, vía Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Un kakapo con huevos en la isla Anchor, en Nueva Zelanda. La isla es conocida por carecer de los tipos de depredadores que amenazan a los loros.

item.title: Word of the Day: embellish
item.description: This word has appeared in 36 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Map: 6.0-Magnitude Earthquake Shakes the Tyrrhenian Sea
item.description: View the location of the quake’s epicenter and shake area.
item.dc:creator: William B. Davis, Madison Dong, Judson Jones, John Keefe, Joey K. Lee and Bea Malsky

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Former Meta A.I. Chief’s Start-Up Is Valued at $3.5 Billion
item.description: Advanced Machine Intelligence Labs, founded by Yann LeCun and other ex-Meta researchers, has raised more than $1 billion from investors.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz

media:content.media:credit: Victor Llorente for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: AMI Labs, which Yann LeCun founded a month ago after leaving Meta, has only a dozen employees.

item.title: Fed Up With High Costs, American Theater Takes a Trip to London
item.description: Skyrocketing budgets and falling profitability have driven a new form of offshoring, with U.S. producers staging shows across the Atlantic.
item.dc:creator: Michael Paulson

media:content.media:credit: Joshua Atkins for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: David Hunter as F. Scott Fitzgerald in a rehearsal for the new American musical “Beautiful Little Fool,” which had an initial run this winter at the Southwark Playhouse in London.

item.title: Latin Americans Already Have a Serious Partner — and It’s Not Trump
item.description: President Trump’s efforts to force the Western Hemisphere into alignment will keep falling short.
item.dc:creator: Oliver Stuenkel

media:content.media:credit: Photo illustration by Philotheus Nisch for The New York Times; source photograph by Eric Lee, via The New York Times

item.title: Un Zelenski cansado y en pie de lucha visita a las tropas en el frente
item.description: El presidente de Ucrania ha adquirido el hábito de visitar a los soldados que se encuentran más cerca de los combates. En esta ocasión, dos periodistas de The New York Times lo acompañaron.
item.dc:creator: Kim Barker and Brendan Hoffman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: El presidente de Ucrania, Volodímir Zelenski, el viernes en Kramatorsk. “Necesitan muchas cosas”, dijo de los soldados. “Y cada día cambian las necesidades”.

item.title: Para China, la ampliación de la guerra pone en peligro activos e inversiones milmillonarios
item.description: El país encontró en Medio Oriente un hogar para sus crecientes inversiones y mercados de acero, vehículos eléctricos y paneles solares. Ahora tiene mucho que perder.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Stevenson and Murphy Zhao
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times
media:content.media:description: El puerto de Beilun en China. Medio Oriente adquirió aún más importancia para China tras la escalada de la rivalidad comercial de China con Estados Unidos.

item.title: As War Comes to Gulf States, Migrant Workers Pay the Highest Price
item.description: Since the American-Israeli attack on Iran began, at least 11 civilians have been killed in oil-rich Gulf countries. All but one of them were foreign nationals.
item.dc:creator: Zia ur-Rehman, Vivian Nereim and Aie Balagtas See
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Christopher Pike/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Smoke rising last week after an explosion in an industrial zone of Fujairah, United Arab Emirates.

item.title: U.S. Solar Installations Fell in 2025 as Trump Attacked Clean Energy
item.description: More solar energy was added to U.S. grids than any other technology, but the amount installed fell by 14 percent, according to a new report.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Penn

media:content.media:credit: Mason Trinca for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A solar field in Riesel, Texas, in 2023. Solar power is projected to account for just over half of the new power projects that will be added this year.

item.title: Corrections: March 10, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Tuesday, March 10, 2026.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Scientists Get a Glimpse Into How Pandemics Start
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Tuesday, March 10, 2026.

item.title: Missile in Deadly Iranian School Strike Appears to Be U.S.-Made, Photos Taken by Iran Show
item.description: Iranian state media posted mangled remnants it claims were from the Feb. 28 attack in Minab. An analysis shows they have the markings of a missile made by American manufacturers
item.dc:creator: Christiaan Triebert, Malachy Browne and John Ismay
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Who Is Balendra Shah, The Rapper Set to Be Nepal’s Next Prime Minister?
item.description: Balendra Shah’s party won a landslide in the election that followed Nepal’s Gen Z revolution. His style is pugnacious.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Beech, Binod Ghimire and Sajal Pradhan

media:content.media:credit: Hannah Reyes Morales for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Balendra Shah campaigning in Kathmandu, Nepal, last month.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for March 10, 2026
item.description: Grab a snack and solve Daniel Bodily’s latest puzzle.
item.dc:creator: Ikechukwu Ufomadu

media:content.media:credit: Brook Mitchell/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Let’s demystify this puzzle.

item.title: Trump Seeks to Calm Oil and Gas Markets but Says Iran War Will Go On
item.description: The president said the U.S. could accompany tankers through the Strait of Hormuz if necessary to keep oil flowing. “We’ve already won in many ways, but we haven’t won enough,” he said.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Benoit Tessier/Reuters
media:content.media:description: Oil tankers sat anchored in Muscat, Oman, on Saturday as Iran vowed to block the Strait of Hormuz amid the conflict.

item.title: Justice Department Says Trump Can Overrule California and Approve Oil Pipeline
item.description: The Justice Department said the president has the legal authority to overrule state regulators and reopen an offshore oil pipeline that was shut down after causing a gigantic oil spill.
item.dc:creator: Soumya Karlamangla
item.category: Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration

media:content.media:credit: Mario Tama/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: The Esther oil and gas platform, located off the coast of Seal Beach, Calif., was shut down last year. But the Trump administration hopes to increase oil production elsewhere off the California coast, including at the site of a 2015 oil spill.

item.title: Two Supreme Court Justices Debate Handling of Trump Emergency Cases
item.description: In a rare joint appearance, Justices Ketanji Brown Jackson and Brett M. Kavanaugh offered sharply different views on how the court should handle emergency requests.
item.dc:creator: Abbie VanSickle

media:content.media:credit: Jason Andrew for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Supreme Court Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson, left, moderator Judge Paul L. Friedman, center, and Supreme Court Justice Brett M. Kavanaugh on Monday, where the two justices differed over how the court is handling emergency requests.

item.title: Trump’s War in Iran, and Rising Gas Prices, Collide With Midterm Agenda
item.description: The attack on Iran has led to a surge in energy prices at a moment when the cost of living is a major issue heading into the fall elections.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Benoit Tessier/Reuters
media:content.media:description: A tanker and a cargo ship in Muscat, Oman, on Monday. The war in Iran has affected the shipping of oil through the Strait of Hormuz and the Gulf of Oman.

item.title: Mamdani Chooses His Words Carefully After Alleged Terror Attack
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani has been spare in his remarks following the attempted bombing at Gracie Mansion and arrests of two men who said they were motivated by ISIS.
item.dc:creator: Dana Rubinstein
item.category: Islamic State in Iraq and Syria (ISIS)

media:content.media:credit: Graham Dickie for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani has been somewhat reserved in his comments following an attack outside Gracie Mansion, issuing two short statements and answering few questions.

item.title: Trump Again Suggests Without Evidence That Iran Struck Elementary School
item.description: After a video showed a Tomahawk missile hitting a naval base next to the school, President Trump claimed Iran possesses such weapons. But the U.S. developed the missile and has sold it only to a few close allies.
item.dc:creator: Shawn McCreesh and John Ismay
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The president suggested without giving evidence on Monday that Iran or another country could have fired the weapon.

item.title: 14 of the Worst Movie Casting Choices
item.description: A new Oscar celebrates the process of matching actor and role. But whether a star is hired for artistic or box office reasons, mismatches can be memorable. Here our favorites from recent decades. What do you think?
item.dc:creator: Manohla Dargis, Stephanie Goodman, Mekado Murphy, Maya Salam and Alissa Wilkinson

item.title: Shells Fired From Lebanon Land West of Syrian Capital, Country’s Military Says
item.description: The Syrian military accused Hezbollah of targeting Syrian army positions outside of Damascus.
item.dc:creator: Dayana Iwaza and Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Khalil Ashawi/Reuters
media:content.media:description: President Ahmed al-Shara at a conference last month in Damascus. On Monday, he called the escalation in the Middle East “an existential threat to the entire region.”

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the Conflict on Monday
item.description: President Trump gave conflicting signals about a possible end to the war with Iran amid wild gyrations in global markets.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump speaking from Doral, Fla., on Monday, initially said the war with Iran was “very complete, pretty much.”

item.title: Murder Charge Is Dropped Against Man Who Fired Gun at Chiefs Super Bowl Parade
item.description: Dominic M. Miller, 20, was sentenced to two years in jail as part of a plea agreement related to the 2024 killing of Lisa Lopez-Galvan. He has already served most of his time.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor

media:content.media:credit: Dominick Williams for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The violence at a 2024 Super Bowl event in Kansas City, Mo., wounded two dozen people, including nine children.

item.title: Epstein’s Remote Zorro Ranch Is Searched by New Mexico Investigators
item.description: Some of the disgraced financier’s victims have said they were trafficked at the property south of Santa Fe, where the nearest neighbors are miles away.
item.dc:creator: Reis Thebault

media:content.media:credit: Paul Ratje for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Lawmakers voted to impanel a bipartisan four-member “truth commission” with subpoena power to investigate Zorro Ranch.

item.title: Iran’s Women’s Soccer Team Offered Asylum by President Trump
item.description: The dissonance between the president’s hard-line immigration policies and his offer of asylum to the athletes was striking. Australia took in five players.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs

media:content.media:credit: Dave Hunt/Australian Associated Press, via Reuters
media:content.media:description: Members of the Iranian women’s soccer team after a match on Australia’s Gold Coast on Sunday.

item.title: Lawmakers Pressure Hochul to Raise Taxes in New York
item.description: The State Assembly and Senate are each calling for the state to raise taxes, piggybacking on a push by Mayor Zohran Mamdani of New York City.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Oreskes

media:content.media:credit: Adam Gray for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Gov. Kathy Hochul and Mayor Zohran Mamdani have become allies, but she has resisted his calls to raise taxes on the rich.

item.title: De 2007: Vilma Espín, rebelde y esposa de Raúl Castro, muere a los 77 años
item.description: Espín fue una idealista de la alta sociedad que luchó junto a Fidel y Raúl Castro en las montañas de Cuba, y la primera dama extraoficial del país durante décadas.
item.dc:creator: Anthony DePalma

media:content.media:credit: PRENSA LATINA, vía Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Vilma Espín hacia 1959. Se convirtió en un símbolo ampliamente reconocido del medio siglo del dominio del poder de la familia Castro.

item.title: Oil Prices and Stocks Swing Wildly as Iran and Israel Launch New Strikes
item.description: Oil prices and stocks initially swung wildly, and Iran’s foreign minister promised “surprises,” calling the strikes against his country “Operation Epic Mistake.”
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Farnaz Fassihi, Richard Pérez-Peña and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: People in Tehran showing their support on Monday for Mojtaba Khamenei, who was selected as Iran’s new supreme leader.

item.title: Oil Prices Will Remain High as Iran War Continues
item.description: While officials look for ways to ease oil shocks, experts say higher prices will likely persist until traffic through the Strait of Hormuz returns.
item.dc:creator: Brad Plumer
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Black smoke filled the skies above Tehran on Sunday morning after Israeli airstrikes struck energy facilities overnight.

item.title: Meteorite Crashes Through Roof in Germany After Fiery Light Show
item.description: The fireball from space was spotted by a network of sky-watching cameras in Belgium, France, Luxembourg, the Netherlands and Germany.
item.dc:creator: Robin George Andrews

item.title: Trade Your Smartphone for an ’80s-Style Digital Watch
item.description: These retro selections may have some new tricks, but they keep things comparatively simple.
item.dc:creator: Jameson Montgomery

item.title: Monti Rock III, Gleefully Untalented ‘Tonight Show’ Favorite, Dies at 86
item.description: He couldn’t sing, dance or tell funny stories. But Johnny Carson loved him and his persona: a D-list star clinging to celebrity.
item.dc:creator: Trip Gabriel

media:content.media:credit: Ron Galella/Ron Galella Collection, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Monti Rock III in 1975. He was a flamboyant dresser, a transparently gay man in the subversive style known as camp in the mid-60s.

item.title: Iran War Overshadows Midterms as Democrats Focus on Economy
item.description: Democrats are focused on the economy, while Republicans wish they were focused on the economy.
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: An explosion after an airstrike in Tehran on Saturday.

item.title: Iran War Maps: Tracking the Mideast Conflict
item.description: Maps show where U.S. and Israel have struck Iran, and where Iran has retaliated.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times
